profile.behavior: Forages on the ground, hopping through and flipping leaf litter for invertebrates; often pauses upright to look around. Its song is clear and flute-like.
field_marks: Warm reddish-brown upperparts, often richest on the head
Bold white eye ring around a large dark eye
White underparts with large, round dark spots concentrated on breast and flanks
Plump, upright thrush shape with a large head and short tail
Straight, slender grayish bill and pink legs
Brown wings and tail with subtly darker feather edging
profile.habitat: Mature deciduous and mixed forest with tall trees, a moderate shrub layer, shaded ground, and abundant leaf litter; also uses lowland tropical forest in the nonbreeding season.

sources.title: Wood Thrush Identification — All About Birds

findings: Identity is correct: the plate depicts Wood Thrush (Hylocichla mustelina), family Turdidae. The scientific name, common name, measurements, habitat, behavior, and stated field marks agree with both live sources.

The illustration correctly shows one head, one straight slender beak, two legs with appropriate anisodactyl perching feet, two wings (one visible in side view), and one tail. It has the characteristic reddish-brown upperparts, bold white eye ring, large dark eye, white underparts with bold dark spotting, and pink legs.

Minor illustration issue: the tail projects somewhat longer and more tapering than the short-tailed profile shown in the reference photos and described by All About Birds; this is not a material identification error.
All visible factual text claims are supported. The plate contains no place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail.

verification_sources.title: Wood Thrush Identification — All About Birds
